directions: Enter via the north research gate and follow signage to the institute annex.
Visitor parking is limited; confirm an appointment before arrival.

outreachTracks.title: Industry translation
outreachTracks.description: Short feasibility reviews for applied collaborations.

outreachTracks.title: Student open labs
outreachTracks.description: Quarterly tours for graduate cohorts and partner universities.

contactInfo.responseWindow: We reply within five working days for general inquiries.

labInfo.mission: The Blida Research Institute advances rigorous, field-tested science across digital systems, biomaterials, energy, and health informatics—prioritizing reproducible methods, open collaboration, and outcomes that translate to regional priorities.
labInfo.name: Blida Research Institute
labInfo.vision: A regional anchor lab where disciplined engineering meets translational research—measured by datasets, deployments, and publications, not slogans.

researchAxes.name: Digital systems & reliability
researchAxes.summary: Resilient software and hardware stacks for instrumentation, control, and data capture—with emphasis on verifiable behavior under stress.

researchAxes.name: Biomaterials & interfaces
researchAxes.summary: Materials science for interfaces that must survive biological and mechanical loads—translating bench protocols into repeatable fabrication steps.

researchAxes.name: Energy systems
researchAxes.summary: Modeling and measurement for hybrid energy systems—linking field telemetry with control policies that respect real grid constraints.

researchAxes.name: Health informatics
researchAxes.summary: Responsible analytics and workflow design for clinical and population datasets—privacy-preserving by default, evaluation-first by habit.

teams.focus: Reliable control software, telemetry integrity, and reproducible benchmarks for instrument-heavy labs.
teams.name: Digital Systems Laboratory
teams.summary: Builds verification-minded pipelines for acquisition, logging, and closed-loop control—so experiments survive noisy environments and long campaigns.

teams.focus: Surface engineering and interface testing for biomedical and industrial coatings.
teams.name: Biomaterials & Interfaces Group
teams.summary: Combines mechanical testing with biological assays to rank coating candidates before scale-up—favoring protocols teams can repeat without heroics.

teams.focus: Hybrid microgrids, dispatch under uncertainty, and field validation of control policies.
teams.name: Energy Systems & Microgrids
teams.summary: Links measurement campaigns to control models—treating every deployment as a dataset that must reconcile with simulation assumptions.

teams.focus: Privacy-aware analytics, workflow instrumentation, and evaluation-first decision support.
teams.name: Health Informatics Unit
teams.summary: Designs pipelines where consent, minimization, and auditability are first-class—so models can be reviewed as engineering artifacts, not black boxes.

members.expertise: Formal methods, real-time systems, fault tolerance
members.name: Prof. Amel Khelifi
members.title: Professor of Computer Science

members.expertise: Privacy engineering, cohort analytics, FHIR pipelines
members.name: Dr. Sofia Mehenni
members.title: Lead, Health Informatics

projects.milestone: Bench harness v1 complete; fault dictionary frozen
projects.summary: A reproducible harness for acquisition pipelines that logs checksums, clock drift, and sensor dropout events for month-long campaigns.
projects.title: Telemetry integrity harness for long-running lab campaigns

projects.milestone: Ranked coating set down-selected after wear + cytotox screen
projects.summary: Pilot line for candidate coatings with paired mechanical and biological screening—aiming for a shortlist that survives scale-up constraints.
projects.title: Wear-resistant coating pilot for instrument interfaces

projects.milestone: Field dataset reconciled with dispatch model (MAPE under target)
projects.summary: Campus-scale microgrid pilot with telemetry-backed dispatch rules and explicit uncertainty bands for day-ahead planning.
projects.title: Campus microgrid pilot with telemetry-backed dispatch

projects.milestone: Privacy review signed off; pipeline in staging
projects.summary: End-to-end cohort analytics pipeline with minimization defaults, role-based access, and documented transformations for audit.
projects.title: Cohort analytics pipeline with minimization-by-default

projects.milestone: Driver stack stabilized on target hardware
projects.summary: Hardened driver stack for legacy acquisition boards—reducing intermittent faults observed during overnight runs.
projects.title: Instrument driver hardening for legacy acquisition boards

publications.abstract: We present a logging discipline for long-running lab campaigns that preserves integrity evidence without overwhelming storage—checksum chains, sparse snapshots, and explicit clock model assumptions.
publications.title: Checksum discipline for long-running acquisition campaigns

publications.abstract: A paired wear and cytotoxicity protocol for ranking coating candidates under realistic contact pressures—designed to reduce false positives from single-metric screens.
publications.title: Paired wear and cytotoxicity screening for instrument coatings

publications.abstract: Field notes from reconciling microgrid telemetry with dispatch models—highlighting where naive forecasts break and how uncertainty bands should enter day-ahead decisions.
publications.journal: Proc. North Africa Energy Systems Workshop
publications.title: Reconciling microgrid telemetry with dispatch models under uncertainty

publications.abstract: A pragmatic checklist for cohort pipelines: minimization defaults, transformation logs, and reviewer-facing evidence that privacy engineering was treated as a first-class subsystem.
publications.title: A privacy checklist for reproducible cohort analytics pipelines

publications.abstract: Short paper on isolating intermittent faults in legacy acquisition paths using layered diagnostics—software traces correlated with power-rail events.
publications.title: Layered diagnostics for intermittent faults in legacy acquisition

publications.abstract: Poster-style summary of corrosion testing loops used to down-select coatings before biological screens—emphasizing repeatability across operators.
publications.title: Corrosion testing loops for coating down-selection

body: The institute is opening a small number of visiting researcher seats for the autumn cycle.
Applications should include a one-page research plan and a pointer to an open artifact (dataset, benchmark, or reproducibility bundle).
news.excerpt: Visiting seats emphasize reproducible artifacts and clear measurement plans—lightweight applications, fast decisions.
news.headline: Visiting researcher seats — autumn cycle

body: A campus annex room has been rewired for instrument-heavy experiments with isolated grounds.
Teams can book dry runs before committing to month-long acquisition windows.
news.excerpt: New bench space supports long campaigns with cleaner power and documented grounding—reducing mysterious dropout events.
news.headline: Instrument bench space upgraded for long campaigns

body: Dispatch models are only as honest as the telemetry that feeds them.
This note shares what we log, what we ignore, and how we reconcile after field weeks.
news.excerpt: Microgrid pilots generated a reconciliation checklist we now require before promoting any model to staging dispatch.
news.headline: What we learned reconciling microgrid telemetry

body: A short guide for students joining cohort-facing projects: consent boundaries, minimization defaults, and where to log transformations.
news.excerpt: Onboarding now includes a privacy engineering walkthrough—so cohort work starts with the right habits, not retroactive patches.
news.headline: Updated onboarding for cohort analytics work
